title: Tapping to Own My Sleep Transformation
subtitle: A daytime tapping practice for claiming the change in rest that was built one day at a time
excerpt: A five-minute daytime tapping practice from Day 30 of a 30-day sleep programme. The script marks the full arc of the journey: from chasing rest to living it. The tapping moves through pride, acknowledgement, and arrival — the recognition that the change that was hoped for is already here.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from the Day 30 daytime tap of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798269649795).

practice.whenToUse: Use on the final day of a 30-day programme, or any time the changes made to sleep patterns deserve to be acknowledged and owned rather than minimised.

content.text: A five-minute daytime tapping practice from Day 30 of a 30-day sleep programme. Day 30 theme: claim rest as a birthright. The daytime tap closes the programme by marking what changed: the nervous system that learned safety, the nights that became familiar, the relationship with the body that shifted from opposition to trust. The reframe names it as arrival — not the end of a programme but the beginning of a new normal.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though this journey took patience, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I doubted along the way, I honour the resilience that brought me here.
Even though I'm still human and learning, I'm open to fully owning the peace I've created.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: I built this calm one day at a time.
Side of eye: I showed up for myself even when it was hard.
Under eye: My body learned safety because I kept showing up.
Under nose: I'm proud of this version of me.
Chin: I'm done chasing rest — I live it now.
Collarbone: I've rewritten my relationship with sleep completely.
Under arm: I wake up different — softer and stronger.
Top of head: I've arrived in the peace I used to hope for.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: Calm is who I am now.
Side of eye: Rest is my natural state.
Under eye: I'm grateful for every step that got me here.
Under nose: I'm proud of what I created in 30 days.
Chin: My body and I are in harmony.
Collarbone: Peace is permanent because I built it.
Under arm: I carry this ease into every area of life.
Top of head: Rest is my birthright and I claim it fully.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy. The script is adapted from the Day 30 daytime tap of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
